description: Hundreds of Polk County youth have experienced the wonder and magic of the 4-H Chick Hatching programming in local classrooms—a highlight of the year for many students. This program explores the life cycle of poultry and teaches about the needs of living things by incubating and hatching chicken eggs. We are excited to bring Chick Hatching at no cost to participants, one of our most popular 4-H youth programs, to our office at 1625 Adventureland Dr. in Altoona on April 16. Our programming is well-suited for curious minds of all ages! Learn about the life cycle of chickens, how chicks hatch and about agriculture and food production. Using an egg from the grocery store you'll get to see how we “candle” the eggs (i.e., shine a bright light through to see if the embryos are developing or never fertilized. At the second station, watch the eggs in our incubator and hopefully watch for any signs of “pipping,” when the chicks begin to peck through their shells or watch newly hatched chicks. Every participant will have the opportunity to make a simple chicken-related craft to take home with them. We will offer 30-minute timeslots with spots for up to 12 people. To help us plan, we are asking members of the public to reserve a 30-minute time slot by April 8 by emailing Polkextoffice@iastate.edu or calling 515-957-5760. All children need to be accompanied by an adult. And we ask participants arrive about 5 minutes before their reserved time slot. Time slots are as follows: Morning Sessions: Group A: 10:30 to 11 AM Group B: 10:45 to 11: 15 AM Group C: 11 to 11:30 AM ( 8 spaces left ) Group D: 11:15 to 11:45 AM Group F: 11:45 AM to 12:15 PM Afternoon Sessions: Group G: 3:45 to 4:15 PM Group I: 4 to 4:30 PM Group J: 4:15 to 4:45 PM Group K: 4:30 to 5 PM Group L: 4:45 to 5:15 PM

event_date: APR 16 "Bill Owens Work and Leisure" Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until April 19, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: Exhibit Runs: December 20, 2025 - April 19, 2026 Bill Owens documented the suburbs in the 1970s, capturing the lives of prosperous, predominantly white Americans at a moment after the cultural shifts of the 1960s but before the dawn of the digital age of computers and video games in the 1980s. His photographs were compiled in the best-selling coffee table books “Leisure” (1972), “Our Kind of People” (1975), and “Working: I Do It For the Money” (1977). The images in this exhibition, taken from all three of these series and shot mainly in California and the Midwest, reveal Owens’ unique comedic eye as he seems to lightly poke fun at his subjects while clearly enjoying their company, finding both the genuine and the absurd in everyday life. The view of America his photography presents is specific and limited to a certain financial, racial, and regional demographic, but in many ways, it also resembles the nostalgic way that era is remembered in pop culture. Now 50 years old, these scenes will look charmingly familiar to many viewers and blasted from the distant past to others. Owens titled his images with quotes from his subjects, allowing their voices to reach us decades later. Through Owens’ lens, they invite us into their homes, share a day at the office, and take us along to several kinds of parties. These photos allow us all to take a moment and join the fun. Bill Owens (American, born 1938) Karate is a highly competitive sport, like tennis or boxing. It’s on one. For $30 a month a kid can get some good mental and physical training. He can learn coordination, balance, timing and self-defense. Livermore, California, from the “Leisure” series, 1974 (printed 1999) Gelatin silver print Des Moines Art Center Permanent Collections: The Jeff Perry Photography Collection given in honor of Myron and Jacqueline Blank, 2024.120

event_date: APR 16 “Christine Rebet: Sound of Time” Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until August 26, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: On view April 11 - August 23: see website for daily hours! This survey exhibition presents five films made between 2015 and 2025 by Paris-based artist Christine Rebet, which play consecutively in the Pamela Bass-Bookey and Harry Bookey Gallery. Rebet has devoted her practice to the act of drawing, creating lush and labor-intensive hand-drawn animations marked by vivid color and lively images that change and morph alongside lyrical voice-over narrations. “I am interested in preserving hand-drawn animation for myself because it is my act, my gesture,” Rebet has explained. “My gesture is to draw. The magic of the hand.” In her films, Rebet imagines and invents visual forms for forgotten rituals, sacred landscapes, and oral histories. Her works are born out of travel and research in places as diverse as Jamaica, the Persian Gulf, Thailand, Southern Iraq, and Haiti, locales where she meets and collaborates with local experts—including musicians, monks, and archaeologists—bringing their knowledge to life through the medium of drawing. The films put historical fact in tension with objects, ideas, and traditions whose forms are unknown, emphasizing the slippery and enigmatic aspects of human knowledge and history. Christine Rebet (French, born 1971) The Fall, 2025 Animation shot on HD, sound 5:19 minutes Courtesy of the artist and Bureau, New York

event_title: “Honey, You’re a Wonderful Model: Maria Lassnig’s Animated Films” Exhibit
event_date: APR 16 “Honey, You’re a Wonderful Model: Maria Lassnig’s Animated Films” Exhibit Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until May 17, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: An immersive installation of moving images, “Honey, You’re a Wonderful Model” presents Austrian artist Maria Lassnig’s (1919-2014) hand-drawn animated films from the 1970s. Lassnig’s animations are populated by a cast of zany characters, frequently including Lassnig herself, who engage in physical comedy and slapstick antics while addressing the complexities of relationships, sexuality, grief, artistic creation, and embodiment. The exhibition, which will feature a 16mm projection, a black box theater, and a series of suspended screens, is the artist’s first solo exhibition in the Midwest and the first exhibition in the United States dedicated to her funny and irreverent animations. Viewers will have the opportunity to see Lassnig’s films alongside eleven drawings created in New York in the 1970s and her 1975 painting “Fischbild / Einen Fisch essen (Fish Picture / Eating a Fish),” shown publicly for the first time since it was acquired by a private collection in the 1970s. Lassnig is well known as a painter of unflinching self-portraits, but she also spent a decade in New York working as an experimental and feminist filmmaker. Her animated films are a unique part of her practice in which she was not only overtly silly but also used comedy to interrogate the human experience. Maria Lassnig (Austrian, 1919-2014) / "Selfportrait", 1971 / (film still) / © Maria Lassnig Foundation / Courtesy sixpackfilm https://desmoinesartcenter.org/art/exhibitions/maria-lassnig/ ART CENTER HOURS: Monday Closed Tuesday 10am - 4pm Wednesday 10am - 4pm Thursday 10am - 7pm Friday 10am - 7pm Saturday 10am - 5pm Sunday 10am - 5pm

description: The 25th anniversary of Authors Visiting in Des Moines (AViD) is upon us! This milestone year features a romance world superstar in Julia Quinn, a literary icon in Tayari Jones, a groundbreaking partnership with the Des Moines Arts Festival and Des Moines Art Center, and much more!. Mark your calendars for these events and get ready for an incredible season of celebrating reading and the arts in Des Moines! MARIA SEMPLE - Des Moines Central Library, downtown. DOORS OPEN: 6:00pm; seating is first-come, first served! Maria Semple is the bestselling author of Where'd You Go, Bernadette (2012), Today Will Be Different (2016), This One is Mine (2008), and the upcoming Go Gentle (2026). Her novels have been translated into 40 languages. Where'd You Go, Bernadette spent over a year on the New York Times bestseller list, was shortlisted for the Women's Prize, and adapted into a 2019 film starring Cate Blanchett. Today Will Be Different became an instant bestseller and was featured on the New York Times Book Review cover. Before writing fiction, Semple wrote for TV shows including Arrested Development , Mad About You , and Ellen . She grew up traveling Europe until her father, screenwriter Lorenzo Semple Jr., created the Batman TV series. After graduating from Barnard College, she moved to Los Angeles for her TV career before turning to novels. Semple now lives in New York and is developing a musical adaptation of Where'd You Go, Bernadette with music by KT Tunstall. Doors open at 6:00 PM - Seating is first-come, first-served. Free parking is available: In the Des Moines Public Library parking garage, located below the library on Grand Ave. On the surface lot on the corner of 10th St. and Grand Ave., across from the library. At the end of the program, there will be a question and answer period. A book signing will follow. Books will be available to purchase before and after the program courtesy of Beaverdale Books.

description: The Drake Road Races stand at the heart of Iowa’s running tradition, offering participants the opportunity to be part of one of the state’s most celebrated and enduring events. Held annually in the lead-up to the Drake Relays, the races bring together athletes, enthusiasts, and community members for a weekend defined by energy, achievement, and shared experience. Featuring 5k, 10k, and half marathon distances, the Drake Road Races are designed to welcome participants of all ability levels. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, training for a larger goal, or simply looking to enjoy an active day in an unforgettable atmosphere, the event provides a race experience that is both accessible and inspiring. Participants enjoy scenic courses that wind through Des Moines, supported by enthusiastic spectators and a vibrant race-day environment. The experience culminates with a finish on the legendary Blue Oval at Drake Stadium — a moment that transforms every stride into something memorable. Crossing that iconic finish line connects runners not only to their own accomplishment, but to a legacy shaped by decades of competition and community pride. More than just a race, the Drake Road Races celebrate fitness, perseverance, and the spirit of participation. They embody the excitement of Relays week while creating meaningful moments for runners, walkers, teams, and supporters alike. Whether you’re a seasoned competitor or a first-time participant, the Drake Road Races deliver an experience that is as rewarding as it is unforgettable.

description: The sizzle returns this spring as the legendary Blue Ribbon Bacon Festival celebrates its 18th edition back where it all began — the iconic High Life Lounge & El Bait Shop! On Saturday, April 18, bacon lovers from across the Midwest will gather for a three-hour celebration of Iowa pork, outrageous food creations, live entertainment, and the unmistakable “porktastic” energy that has defined the festival since 2008. This year’s festival offers attendees all-you-can-eat bacon, a lineup of seven bacon-inspired food items, live music, and classic BaconFest antics — all included with a $60 ticket. Guests will enjoy premium bacon from some of Iowa’s finest producers, including Des Moines Bacon Company, Berkwood Farms, and City Meat Market, each representing the rich heritage and quality of Iowa pork. Bacon-Inspired Food Lineup: A mix of returning favorites and new creations will be served festival-style throughout the venue, including: • Berkwood Farms Pork Burger • Fong’s Bacon Pizza Slice • Bacon Cotton Candy • Maple Bacon Donut • Pineapple Pork Belly Popsicle from BW Outdoors • Speciality Item from Chef Dom Ianarelli of Prime & Providence • Party Balls from JR’s Southpork Ranch • Bacon Breakfast Balls from Jewell Market Schedule of Events: 11:00 AM – Doors Open 12:00 PM – Live Acoustic Set by Owen Justice 1:30 PM – Bacon Eating Contest A $60 admission includes an official festival T-shirt, all-you-can-eat bacon, seven bacon-inspired food items, a commemorative BRBF koozie, Barn Town Brewery beer, and High Noon drink specials available throughout the event. Founded in Des Moines, the Blue Ribbon Bacon Festival has become one of America’s most celebrated and flavorful events dedicated to all things bacon. Known for its over-the-top atmosphere, quirky traditions, and fierce loyalty to Iowa pork, the festival has expanded globally with editions in Japan, Iceland, and beyond all while keeping its heart firmly rooted in the Midwest. The 18th Blue Ribbon Bacon Festival marks a return to home turf, honoring the original spirit of community, creativity, and bacon-loving joy.

event_title: Moors & McCumber at Progressive Voices Concert Series
event_date: APR 18 Moors & McCumber at Progressive Voices Concert Series First Unitarian Church of Des Moines QUICK VIEW
description: Tickets at humanitix.com A vocal harmony duo fifteen years in the making, Moors & McCumber have done more than just endear themselves to fans - they’ve built a genuine community. Bridging the gap between classic folk rock and present-day Americana, they write heartfelt songs directly inspired by life’s experiences. With their latest record, released in 2023, 'Wired to the Moon,” they have created some of their most compelling work to date. The sound of Moors & McCumber is adventurous, eclectic, and endlessly creative. Borrowing from rock, pop, blues, folk, and world music, the two multi-instrumentalists defy common logic and embrace spontaneity to bring their stories and songs to life. Their strong vocal harmonies and unconventional instrumentation combine to create a mesmerizing experience. At the center of it all is their evocative songwriting. While “more than the sum of their parts” has become a bit of a cliché, there is no better phrase to describe Moors & McCumber. James is a gifted singer and songwriter, while Kort specializes in creating vocal harmonies and captivating arrangements through inventive instrumentation. With their voices combined, they create a truly special sound. As Grammy award-winning producer Lloyd Maines says, “They bring it all to the stage and deliver in a big way."

description: The Insect Expo is a celebration featuring live insects and interactive displays where entomologists connect with the Greater Des Moines community to inspire curiosity and discover the value of insects in our lives. This free, family-friendly, science-based event is open to the general public and will occur in the Des Moines Marriott Downtown Exhibit Hall. Activities Include: • Live insects and arthropods • Hands-on microscope and specimen activities • Insect-themed arts, crafts & games • Educational materials from entomologists • STEM activities hosted by student groups and community partners The Insect Expo is a science outreach event hosted by the North Central Branch of the Entomological Society of America, and is occurring during our annual meeting. Entomologists from around the Upper Midwest will be sharing their knowledge and love of insects with attendees of this Sunday afternoon event. Come learn about insects, and ways you can conserve and protect these important organisms essential to Iowa agriculture! For more information, you can contact Kirk Larsen at larsenkj@luther.edu, Nathan Brockman at mantisnb@iastate.edu of Reiman Gardens, or Ben Knowlton at Ben.Knowlton@uky.edu.

event_date: APR 20 Drake Relays Pole Vault Jordan Creek Town Center QUICK VIEW
description: For the second time in a three-year span, Jordan Creek Town Center will host an elite-level pole vault competition indoors on Monday, April 20, beginning at 6:00pm, as part of the 116th Drake Relays. The men's field for the Vault at Jordan Creek will include the current American record-holder and an Olympic silver medalist going head-to-head while the women's division is set to feature the top two athletes in the world. "Vault at Jordan Creek is such a special showcase of athletic excellence and community engagement, and we're excited to see this elite indoor pole vault competition return," Franklin P. Johnson Director of the Drake Blake Boldon said. "Hosting world-class athletes in a unique indoor venue highlights the very best of our sport while inspiring young athletes and giving fans a front-row seat to unforgettable performances. The return of this event strengthens our commitment to delivering exceptional experiences for competitors, spectators, and the entire community." The women's field at Vault at Jordan Creek will showcase a handful of superb vaulters including the top two ranked athletes in the world. Katie Moon, the current world No. 1, will be in Jordan Creek Town Center. Moon boasts the second-best American jump of all-time at 4.95m/16-2.75. She was a 2021 Olympic champion in Tokyo and more recently took the silver medal in Paris. Moon will go head-to-head with world No. 2 Sandi Morris, who was announced yesterday as a 2026 Drake Relays Athletes Hall of Fame inductee. Morris holds the American record and has captured six Relays titles in 2016-19, 2021, and the Capital Square Pole Vault crown in 2016. She was an Olympic silver medalist that same year and earned four World Championships during her career. The women's field is also highlighted by 2026 US Indoor runner-up Emily Grove, who qualified for the 2025 World Indoor Championships. Grove has a long history of jumping at Drake both as a professional competitor and collegiate star at the University of South Dakota. Reigning men's division champion KC Lightfoot will be returning to Des Moines to defend his Vault at Jordan Creek crown. Lightfoot currently holds the American men's record at 6.07m/19-11, a mark set in 2023. He finished fourth at the Tokyo Olympics in 2021, the same year he won the NCAA Indoor title while at Baylor University. Lightfoot is tied atop the collegiate all-time list with current world record holder and Olympic champion Mondo Duplantis. Six-time U.S. champion Chris Nilsen will be part of the loaded men's field. Nilsen, like Emily Grove, was a standout at South Dakota and ranks third all-time among American men with a 6.05m/19-10.25 indoor leap. Nilsen is a 2-time Olympian with a silver medal in 2021. He also was a Drake Relays champion in 2022, his last visit to Des Moines. Zach Bradford is also among the highlights of the men's field. Bradford has qualified for World Outdoor Championships twice and won the US Indoor title in 2026. A selection of the elite vaulters will also compete outdoors at Drake Stadium during the afternoon session on Saturday, April 25. 2026 marks the first time in two years that the Drake Relays has hosted an indoor pole vault event at the Jordan Creek Town Center. The 2024 edition coincided with the mall's 20th anniversary and marked the end of a 10-year hiatus of the event. The Drake Relays will partner with Catch Des Moines to host the Vault at Jordan Creek. "Drake Relays is one of our city's most iconic and impactful events," said Trina Flack, president and CEO of Catch Des Moines. We are excited to partner on the Vault at Jordan Creek Town Center, which allows us to showcase a vibrant fun area of our community to visitors while giving residents a fun and unique way to experience the excitement of the Relays." Vault at Jordan Creek is open to the public; no ticket necessary. All session tickets for Drake Relays are on sale here. A ticket for the Saturday afternoon session is required to watch the elite outdoor pole vault competition at Drake Stadium.
address: 101 Jordan Creek Parkway, West Des Moines, IA 50266

description: The Psychology of Serial Killers on April 22 | 7:30 pm Tickets on sale starting January 23 @ 10:00 AM Reserved Seating: $20, $25, $39, $49 The Psychology of Serial Killers is a gripping masterclass that dives deep into one of the most disturbing and fascinating subjects of our time. Renowned clinical psychologist and criminal expert Dr. Rachel Toles explores the terrifying minds of the world’s most infamous serial killers, revealing how many lived undetected among their neighbors for years before being apprehended. In this intense and thought-provoking presentation, Dr. Toles confronts the questions we’re both afraid and compelled to ask: Who are these notorious killers? How does a seemingly ordinary person become a mass murderer? Why are we so fascinated by them? Why are most serial killers white males? And why do some women fall in love with serial killers? Through expert analysis and real-world case studies, Dr. Toles leads audiences on a chilling journey into the psychology of these dark individuals, uncovering the patterns, myths, and unsettling truths behind their crimes. Dr. Rachel Toles is a forensic psychologist specializing in extreme violence, serial homicide, and the cultural narratives that shape public understanding of criminal behavior. She conducts violence risk assessments, provides expert testimony, and consults on high-profile criminal cases. Her research focuses on the intersection of psychology, media representation, and societal perception of violent crime. Dr. Toles consults across television and streaming, advising on behavioral realism, offender psychology, and character development. She works with major film and television projects, including Dick Wolf Productions, where she advises on the psychological accuracy of crime narratives, character behavior, and threat escalation. Her media credits also include HBO Max, Investigation Discovery, Peacock, Apple TV, NBCUniversal, ABC News Nightline (Impact x Hulu), Oxygen, and Sony Pictures Television. She speaks nationally and internationally on the psychology of homicide, pathways to violence, and the warning behaviors that often appear long before an act becomes visible.

description: The 11th Annual Sarah Frank & Jack Balcombe Dream Project Presents "For Peter Pan on Her 70th Birthday" – by Sarah Ruhl Directed by Thomas D. Perrine Featuring Arlene McAtee – Dreamer Presented: April 24 through May 10 Poisoned apples. Glass slippers. Who needs ’em?! When Ann thinks of her father, she immediately remembers playing Peter Pan in her hometown theater in Davenport Iowa, particularly when he used to bring her flowers after her performance. Her memory is jogged by the fact that she and her four siblings are in their father’s hospital room during his final moments. His death sparks a conversational wake that includes everything from arguments over politics to when each sibling realized that they grew up. A loving look at a family’s view of death, life and the allure of never growing up.

event_date: APR 29 Blood on the Clocktower Rook Room Game Lounge and Cafe QUICK VIEW
description: When the clock strikes midnight at our upcoming Blood on the Clocktower event, there’s a very good chance the person sitting next to you may not make it out alive. Join us on the last Wednesday of each month from 5:30 to 9 PM for a night of treachery, deduction, and delightfully dramatic accusations as The Rook Room Game Lounge & Cafe transforms into the perfect stage for the wildly popular social deduction game Blood on the Clocktower. For this event, we’re diving headfirst into the Trouble Brewing scenario, the beloved beginner-friendly setup where demons lurk, minions scheme, and villagers try very, very hard not to end up dead. No experience required – just bring a willingness to play your role, spin a few tales, and maybe stab your neighbors in the back…metaphorically, of course (the real pitchforks stay at home, villagers). We will be holding a village festival starting at 5:30 PM. This is your chance to grab something to drink and eat while you mix and mingle with your fellow villagers. Get to know your fellow players before all hell breaks loose when the actual gameplay begins at 6:00 PM. This is part of the experience, so all are encouraged to attend the festivities. Tickets are $15 and unlock your seat at the table. Event-exclusive drinks, including the Stroke of Midnight cocktail or its equally devious non-alcoholic counterpart, the Backstabber, will be available only during the event. Because what’s a night of betrayal without something tasty in your glass? To heighten the mood, we’re going full immersion – candlelight, spooky tunes, and eerie decor will set the stage. Two storytellers will be running two games simultaneously, so we can get as many townsfolk and evil minions involved as possible. Each game runs about 60–120 minutes, which means you’ll have time to play, plot, and maybe even grab another drink or bite to eat since we’ll have our full food, beverage, and cafe menus available. New to Blood on the Clocktower? No problem! While we recommend checking out the basics beforehand, our storytellers will guide you through every twist, turn, and dramatic gasp. What we do ask is that you come ready to commit to your role and keep the spirit of the game alive for yourself and your fellow doomed townsfolk. This is an 18+ event and contains depictions of murder that may be upsetting to some audiences. Space is also limited, and if you’ve been to any of our past events, you know seats vanish faster than a villager accused of witchcraft. So grab your ticket, sharpen your wits, and prepare to whisper in corners like a guilty minion plotting their next move. After all, in Blood on the Clocktower, everyone has a role to play…and nobody can be trusted.

description: Brigham Young University presents Vocal Point and Noteworthy on April 29, 2026 @ 7:00 pm Reserved Seating: $29, $35, $40 They have percussion without drums. They have rhythm without a bass. The only instruments on stage are the fourteen voices of the nationally renowned a cappella groups Vocal Point and Noteworthy. Brigham Young University’s premier a cappella ensemble captivates audiences throughout the world with their stunning, high-energy performances and elaborate music videos. With a wide range of vocal possibilities — and seeming impossibilities — these family-friendly crowd-pleasers have it all: rock, pop, country, jazz and R&B. Noteworthy and Vocal Point take the songs you are familiar with, revamp them and deliver a new rendition, complete with all the complex instrumentation done entirely with their mouths. Each ensemble member displays musicality, creativity and stage experience — which results in original, skillful and spirited performances. “Well, that’s about the most professional group at the collegiate level anywhere,” says Deke Sharon, founder of the Contemporary A Cappella Society of America (CASA) and vocal producer of NBC’s The Sing-Off. Vocal Point and Noteworthy originate in the office of Performing Arts Management at Brigham Young University in Provo, Utah. BYU is one of the nation’s largest private universities with an enrollment of more than 30,000 full time students from throughout the United States and 100 foreign countries.

description: Join us for a plant sale and seed swap in the Middlebrook Farm Stand! Community Seed Swap Guidelines: • The seed swap will take place from 10:00 – 11:00 AM on Saturday, May 2. • All seed swaps should be 1 for 1. • Seeds should be packaged in paper envelopes or plastic baggie, individually labeled. • Suggested quantity per package is 10-20 larger seeds, 20-30 pepper or tomato or ⅛ -½ tsp of smaller seeds. • Packages labeled with seed name, variety name, year collected (should be in the last five years), and swapper’s first name. If applicable, include size, color, growth habits. • You may also swap opened commercial seed packets dated 2022-2025 with at least half the original contents and taped closed.

description: Jimmy Failla: Everybody Calm Down Tour May 2, 2026 | 7:00 pm Reserved Seating: $49, $59, $69 Jimmy Failla is a comedian, television host, radio host, TV contributor, and trophy husband. He has his own show on Fox News, “Saturday Night with Jimmy Failla,” and he’s on the nationally syndicated talk show, “Fox Across America.” His latest book “Cancel Culture” has made it to the NY Times best seller list. Jimmy can be heard on over 150 radio stations, Fox Nation, and the Fox News App. He lives on Long Island, New York with his wife Jen and his son Lincoln. When he’s not opiating the masses with his wit and wisdom, he’s gambling on youth sports or bottoming out in his backyard with a rotating cast of showbiz figures. Failla was raised in Levittown, New York. A former New York City cab driver, Failla was named Outstanding Male Comedian of the year at the 2014 New York City Nightlife Awards. His one-hour standup special, “State Of The Union,” is currently streaming on Amazon Prime. He has also made multiple stand-up appearances on AXS TV’s “Gotham Comedy Live.” He played himself on “The Jim Gaffigan Show” on TV LAND. In addition, he appears regularly on “Kennedy” on the Fox Business Channel as well as “America’s Newsroom,” and has made multiple appearances on “The Greg Gutfeld Show,” “The Ingraham Angle,” “Watters World,” and “The Story with Marth MacCallum.” He has also appeared on “Strange Inheritance: Unpacked” as well as “Redeye” on Fox News. He has served as spokesman for the Flipps Mobile App. In addition, he is the head writer for A-List Comedy, a national comedy service that supplies topical humor and audio sketches to over two hundred radio stations.

description: The 25th anniversary of Authors Visiting in Des Moines (AViD) is upon us! She is the writer behind The Daily Tay blog and founder and creative director of online clothing stores, The Taylor Wolfe Shop and Chilly Wolfe. Her work has been featured on the Today show, the Dodo, and more. Her new book, Just a Busy Season , is an honest and hilarious essay collection on parenting young children (she has two young children, Birdie and Goldie), navigating social anxiety, and being a millennial woman. Wolfe's first book, Birdie & Harlow, came out in 2023. The book was a memoir about her bond with her dog, Harlow, and how her intense bond with her dog and what it taught her about family and motherhood. Admission is free. Doors will open at approximately 6:20 pm. Seating is first-come, first-served. Learn about parking at the DSM Book Festival. At the end of the program, there will be a question and answer period. description: Theology on Tap is a monthly, casual conversation about life, faith, and the questions that matter most. We gather at Lucky Horse Beer and Burgers for open, respectful discussion in a relaxed setting. This is not a lecture or traditional Bible study. There’s no homework, no pressure to speak, and no expectation to believe a certain way. This is an open and affirming space. LGBTQ+ people, allies, and those exploring faith from many perspectives are fully welcome and celebrated here. Order a drink if you’d like, pull up a chair, and join the conversation at whatever level feels comfortable to you. All are welcome, including those who are spiritually curious, unsure what they believe, have been hurt by church, or have stepped away from religion in the past. Free to attend. Food and drinks available for purchase. Come as you are. Bring your questions. You belong here.

event_date: MAY 06 Shaun Johnson Sings Country - 90's Edition The Ingersoll QUICK VIEW
description: Dining Time: 5:30pm; Showtime: 7:30pm Shaun Sings Country – ’90s Edition is a new live show centered on the artists and songs that defined 1990s country music, including music made famous by Alan Jackson, George Strait, Tim McGraw, and Travis Tritt. The show is led by Shaun Johnson, an Iowa native, Emmy® Award winner, and longtime frontman of Tonic Sol-fa, with whom he toured nationally for more than two decades. During that same period, Shaun lived and worked in Nashville— writing songs, touring, and collaborating with many artists from the era. His work recently notched a Top 5 album on the Billboard® charts as well as appearances on both HBO and NBC. Rather than a tribute act, the performance pairs well-known songs with firsthand context from Shaun’s time in the industry, offering insight into the music, the songwriting, and the culture of country music during its commercial peak. Shaun is joined by a seasoned band featuring David Stanoch on drums, Louis Stanoch on guitar, and Jeremy Boettcher on bass. Together, they present a focused concert experience rooted in musicianship and storytelling, with an emphasis on the songs themselves. Your ticket includes our house greens salad with choice of dressing, basket of dinner rolls with butter, and a selection of entrée options, along with coffee, tea, and water. All other drinks such as alcohol, soda, starters, entrée upgrades and desserts are available for purchase during your Ingersoll dining experience.

event_title: Common Ground Film Screening & Farmer Panel
event_date: MAY 08 Common Ground Film Screening & Farmer Panel Wake Up Iowa Coffee QUICK VIEW
description: Watch a film about our food system and hear from a farmer on how they adapt their practices to meet these challenges Common Ground is the highly anticipated sequel to the juggernaut success documentary, Kiss the Ground, which touched over 1 billion people globally and inspired the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) to put $20 billion toward soil health. By fusing journalistic expose’ with deeply personal stories from those on the front lines of the food movement, Common Ground unveils a dark web of money, power, and politics behind our broken food system. The film reveals how unjust practices forged our current farm system in which farmers of all colors are literally dying to feed us. The film profiles a hopeful and uplifting movement of white, black, and indigenous farmers who are using alternative “regenerative” models of agriculture that could balance the climate, save our health, and stabilize America’s economy – before it’s too late. We will watch the film Common Ground, followed by a panel with Farm Baby to discuss their farming practices and ways you can support a more regenerative food system! Registration is required. The $10 class fee directly supports the farmers speaking at this event. Please email admin@cultivatefoodconnections.org with any questions!

event_date: MAY 14 The Roommate The Ingersoll QUICK VIEW
description: Sharon needs a roommate. Robyn needs to disappear. When the two women become unlikely housemates, their lives quickly take unexpected turns. Sharon’s quiet routine is disrupted by Robyn’s mysterious past and bold outlook on life, and what begins as a simple living arrangement soon spirals into something far more thrilling—and dangerous. The Roommate by Jen Silverman is a deliciously dark, off-beat comedy about second acts, unexpected friendships, and the intoxicating allure of reinvention. Directed by Maxwell Schaeffer, this production stars award-winning Iowa actors Kellie Kramer (Sharon) and Kristin Larson (Robyn). Cast: Kellie Kramer (Sharon) Kellie Kramer is an award-winning Iowa actor and singer whose credits include roles at the Des Moines Playhouse in A Streetcar Named Desire (Blanche DuBois), Mamma Mia! (Donna Sheridan), and The 39 Steps . Other area stage credits include One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest (Nurse Ratched) at the Playhouse, Next to Normal (Diana) at StageWest, Prelude to a Kiss (Rita) at Tallgrass Theatre Company, and Lizzie Borden: The Musical (Lizzie) at History Boys Theatre Company. Kellie also hosted the Emmy-nominated Iowa Outdoors on Iowa PBS. She proudly considers herself a bona fide road-tripper and fried potato aficionado. Kristin Larson (Robyn) Kristin Larson is the professor and chair of Theatre Arts at Grand View University, where she is fortunate to create theatre with talented and creative students. She holds a BFA in Acting from Southern Oregon University and an MFA in Directing from the University of Montana. Kristin recently directed Men on Boats and Once at the Des Moines Playhouse. Other stage credits include Other Desert Cities at Tallgrass Theatre Company, Time Stands Still with StageWest Theatre, and The Beauty Queen of Leenane with Mooncoin Entertainment. Though she spends much of her artistic life directing, Kristin is thrilled for this opportunity to step into a role and onto the stage with this exciting team at The Ingersoll Dinner Theater. Your ticket includes our house greens salad with choice of dressing, basket of dinner rolls with butter, and a selection of entrée options, along with coffee, tea, and water. All other drinks such as alcohol, soda, starters, entrée upgrades and desserts are available for purchase during your Ingersoll dining experience.

event_date: MAY 14 AVID Author Series: Shannon Chakraborty Des Moines Public Library QUICK VIEW
description: The 25th anniversary of Authors Visiting in Des Moines (AViD) is upon us! This milestone year features a romance world superstar in Julia Quinn, a literary icon in Tayari Jones, a groundbreaking partnership with the Des Moines Arts Festival and Des Moines Art Center, and much more!. Mark your calendars for these events and get ready for an incredible season of celebrating reading and the arts in Des Moines! SHANNON CHAKRABORTY - Des Moines Central Library, downtown. DOORS OPEN: 6:00pm; seating is first-come, first served! Shannon Chakraborty is the critically acclaimed, New York Times bestselling author of The Daevabad Trilogy and The Adventures of Amina al-Sirafi . Her work has been translated into over a dozen languages and nominated for the Hugo, Locus, World Fantasy, Crawford, and Astounding awards. The second novel in her Amina Al-Sirafi series, The Tapestry of Fate , is being released on May 12. Doors open at 6:00 PM. Seating is first-come, first-served. Free parking is available: In the Des Moines Public Library parking garage, located on Grand Ave. On the surface lot on the corner of 10th St. and Grand Ave., across from the library. At the end of the program, there will be a question and answer period. A book signing will follow. Books will be available to purchase before and after the program courtesy of Beaverdale Books.

description: Ride strong, celebrate together, and make unforgettable memories at the Pigtails Ride – a fun, springtime 45-mile ride celebrating women, friendship, and strength along the stunning High Trestle Trail. Ride with GPS route link: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/54269406 Your Registration Includes: • Access to the pre-ride packet pickup party Friday evening at Firetrucker Brewery with drink specials • Breakfast at the start of the ride • A branded wristband to get you access to the food and fun on the ride • Custom swag items • Lunch provided at the Flat Tire Lounge stop party with live music • A professional photo on High Trestle Trail bridge • SAG and safety support on the ride • Support to the Iowa Bicycle Coalition's bike advocacy mission

event_date: MAY 16 SCI Solar Party Science Center of Iowa QUICK VIEW
description: SCI Solar Parties offer visitors a safe, up‑close look at our nearest star! Join SCI staff and volunteers on the first and third Saturdays of each month during the Downtown Des Moines Farmers Market as we explore the Sun using specially filtered solar telescopes. Guests can observe features like sunspots, granulation, prominences, and other dynamic solar activity, all while learning how different filters reveal different layers and wavelengths of the Sun. These free, drop‑in events take place from 9–11 AM on the north side of the Science Center near the main entrance. No tickets or registration are required.
address: 401 W Martin Luther King Jr. Parkway, Des Moines, IA 50309
